subparts.text: An investment has the following annual returns over three years: 8%, 12%, and 10%, measure the risk of the investment. 1
Diagrammatically present the Security Market Line . 4
An investor's portfolio has an average return of 12% with a risk-free rate of 3%. If the standard deviation of the portfolio's returns is 15%, calculate the Sharpe Ratio. 4

questions.question_text: Explain the concepts of systematic and unsystematic risks. Discuss how beta, standard deviation, and coefficient of variation help measure risk. 1
Describe the EIC framework in detail. How do macroeconomic variables influence stock prices in the Indian context? Provide real-life examples. 1
Discuss the assumptions and limitations of DDM. Illustrate with examples how DDM is used to value stocks of companies with stable dividend payouts. 2
Explain the significance of price and volume indicators in technical analysis. Provide an overview of moving averages and their role in identifying trends. 2
Discuss the concept of the efficient frontier and the importance of diversification in portfolio construction. 3

An investor is constructing a portfolio with two assets: Asset A and Asset B. The details of the assets are as follows:  Expected return of Asset A: 10%, Standard deviation: 8%  Expected return of Asset B: 15%, Standard deviation: 12%  Correlation coefficient between Asset A and Asset B: 0.3  Portfolio weights: 60% in Asset A and 40% in Asset B Calculate: 1. The expected return of the portfolio. 2. The risk of the portfolio. 3 2
Describe how Sharpe, Treynor, and Jensen measures are used to evaluate mutual fund performance, with examples. 4

A stock has a beta of 1.2. The risk-free rate is 5%, and the expected return on the market portfolio is 12%. Calculate the expected return of the stock using the Capital Asset Pricing Model . If the actual return of the stock is 15%, determine whether the stock is overvalued or undervalued, Comment. 4
